mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of business and management principles involved in strategic planning, resource allocation, human resources modeling, leadership technique, production methods, and coordination of people and resources.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of strategic planning methodologies for data and AI initiatives, including vision development, objective setting, multi-year roadmap construction, and alignment with enterprise business strategy.
mappings.notes: O*NET element is broader (covers all business management); WIDAI LS-K-001 is specific to data/AI strategic planning. O*NET is a superset. Secondary matches: LS-K-003 (organizational design), LS-K-005 (board governance).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of administrative and office procedures and systems such as word processing, managing files and records, stenography and transcription, designing forms, and workplace terminology.
mappings.notes: General office administration is outside WIDAI scope. WIDAI covers data/AI professional competencies, not administrative office procedures. No gap — intentionally out of scope.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of economic and accounting principles and practices, the financial markets, banking, and the analysis and reporting of financial data.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data monetization models, including direct data licensing, data-as-a-service, embedded analytics, and indirect value creation through operational efficiency, and methods for quantifying data asset value.
mappings.notes: O*NET covers broad economics/accounting/finance. WIDAI LS-K-010 covers data monetization specifically. Partial overlap in financial analysis concepts. Secondary: RM-K-001 (risk quantification uses financial concepts), LS-K-008 (operational budgeting). O*NET is much broader.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of principles and methods for showing, promoting, and selling products or services. This includes marketing strategy and tactics, product demonstration, sales techniques, and sales control systems.
mappings.notes: Sales and marketing is outside WIDAI scope. While data products have go-to-market considerations, WIDAI does not define sales/marketing competencies. No gap — intentionally out of scope.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of principles and processes for providing customer and personal services. This includes customer needs assessment, meeting quality standards for services, and evaluation of customer satisfaction.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of self-service data platform design principles, including data marketplace concepts, data catalog usability, access request workflows, and user experience considerations that drive adoption.
mappings.notes: Partial overlap: WIDAI covers internal 'customer' service (data consumers as customers of data platforms). O*NET covers broader customer service. The intersection is in needs assessment and service quality. Secondary: OP-S-004 (service catalog management).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of principles and procedures for personnel recruitment, selection, training, compensation and benefits, labor relations and negotiation, and personnel information systems.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of talent acquisition, retention, and development strategies specific to data and AI roles, including market compensation dynamics, skill assessment methodologies, career path design, and the organizational conditions that attract and retain scarce technical talent.
mappings.notes: O*NET covers all HR. WIDAI LS-K-002 covers data/AI talent management specifically. Strong overlap in recruitment, selection, training, compensation for technical roles. Secondary: LS-K-013 (workforce planning), LS-S-002 (talent pipeline building).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of raw materials, production processes, quality control, costs, and other techniques for maximizing the effective manufacture and distribution of goods.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data quality dimensions (accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness, validity, uniqueness) and how each dimension affects downstream analytics, reporting, and decision-making.
mappings.notes: Functional overlap: production quality control concepts parallel data quality management. O*NET focuses on physical production; WIDAI focuses on data production pipelines. The quality control and process optimization principles intersect.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of techniques and equipment for planting, growing, and harvesting food products (both plant and animal) for consumption, including storage/handling techniques.
mappings.notes: Entirely outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of circuit boards, processors, chips, electronic equipment, and computer hardware and software, including applications and programming.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals, including data types, control structures, functions, object-oriented programming, standard library modules, and the Python ecosystem for data processing (pandas, NumPy) and machine learning (scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow).
mappings.notes: O*NET covers broad computing knowledge including hardware. WIDAI TF domain covers software, programming, and data infrastructure specifically. Multiple WIDAI KSAs fall within this O*NET element: TF-K-001 (Python), TF-K-002 (SQL/RDBMS), TF-K-005 (cloud platforms), TF-K-008 (distributed computing), TF-K-013 (networking). O*NET is a superset encompassing all of these plus hardware.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of the practical application of engineering science and technology. This includes applying principles, techniques, procedures, and equipment to the design and production of various goods and services.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data architecture design principles, including data modeling techniques (conceptual, logical, physical), schema design patterns (star, snowflake, Data Vault), and the trade-offs between different modeling approaches for analytical versus operational workloads.
mappings.notes: O*NET is about engineering broadly. WIDAI covers data/AI engineering specifically. Intersection in design principles, technical architecture, and systems engineering. Secondary: DA-K-002 (data integration), DA-K-004 (platform architecture).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of design techniques, tools, and principles involved in production of precision technical plans, blueprints, drawings, and models.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data architecture design principles, including data modeling techniques (conceptual, logical, physical), schema design patterns (star, snowflake, Data Vault), and the trade-offs between different modeling approaches for analytical versus operational workloads.
mappings.notes: Functional overlap: design principles apply to data architecture and system design. O*NET focuses on physical/visual design; WIDAI on data system design. The functional outcome — creating structured plans for complex systems — intersects.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of materials, methods, and the tools involved in the construction or repair of houses, buildings, or other structures such as highways and roads.
mappings.notes: Entirely outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of machines and tools, including their designs, uses, repair, and maintenance.
mappings.notes: Physical mechanical knowledge outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of arithmetic, algebra, geometry, calculus, statistics, and their applications.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of descriptive statistics, including measures of central tendency, dispersion, distribution shape, and correlation, and how to interpret each in a business context.
mappings.notes: O*NET covers all mathematics. WIDAI covers statistics and quantitative methods extensively: AB-K-001 (descriptive stats), AB-K-004 (advanced analytical methods), AB-K-008 (statistical modeling), AB-K-009 (causal inference), AI-K-003 (probability/statistics for ML). The union of WIDAI math-related KSAs is a subset of O*NET's broad mathematics knowledge.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge and prediction of physical principles, laws, their interrelationships, and applications to understanding fluid, material, and atmospheric dynamics, and mechanical, electrical, atomic and sub-atomic structures and processes.
mappings.notes: Physics knowledge outside WIDAI scope. No gap — not a data/AI professional competency.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of the chemical composition, structure, and properties of substances and of the chemical processes and transformations that they undergo.
mappings.notes: Chemistry knowledge outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of plant and animal organisms, their tissues, cells, functions, interdependencies, and interactions with each other and the environment.
mappings.notes: Biology knowledge outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of human behavior and performance; individual differences in ability, personality, and interests; learning and motivation; psychological research methods; and the assessment and treatment of behavioral and affective disorders.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of change management frameworks and organizational behavior principles relevant to data and AI transformations, including resistance patterns, adoption drivers, stakeholder engagement models, and cultural assessment methods.
mappings.notes: Partial overlap: organizational psychology, behavior, and motivation concepts underpin WIDAI change management and leadership KSAs. O*NET covers broad psychology including clinical. WIDAI LS-K-009 covers organizational behavior subset. Secondary: LS-S-005 (stakeholder engagement), AG-K-009 (AI literacy — learning/cognition).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of group behavior and dynamics, societal trends and influences, human migrations, ethnicity, cultures, and their history and origins.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
mappings.notes: Weak intersection: societal dynamics and group behavior inform AI fairness and bias analysis. O*NET covers broad sociology. WIDAI covers AI societal impact through governance lens. The intersection is in understanding how group dynamics and societal structures relate to algorithmic fairness.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of principles and methods for describing the features of land, sea, and air masses, including their physical characteristics, locations, interrelationships, and distribution of plant, animal, and human life.
mappings.notes: Geography knowledge outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of the information and techniques needed to diagnose and treat human injuries, diseases, and deformities.
mappings.notes: Medical knowledge outside WIDAI scope. WIDAI covers healthcare data compliance (HIPAA) but not medical practice itself. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of principles, methods, and procedures for diagnosis, treatment, and rehabilitation of physical and mental dysfunctions, and for career counseling and guidance.
mappings.notes: Therapy/counseling outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of principles and methods for curriculum and training design, teaching and instruction for individuals and groups, and the measurement of training effects.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data literacy program design, including competency assessment, curriculum development, delivery modalities, and measurement of literacy improvement across organizational roles.
mappings.notes: Strong intersection. WIDAI LS-K-012 covers training design specifically for data/AI literacy. O*NET covers training design broadly. The training design methodology concepts overlap significantly. Secondary: LS-S-007 (mentoring), OP-K-010 (community of practice facilitation).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of the structure and content of the English language including the meaning and spelling of words, and rules of composition and grammar.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
mappings.notes: Weak intersection: English language competency underlies all WIDAI communication KSAs (LS-S-006, AB-S-002, LS-S-005) but WIDAI does not define language competency as a standalone KSA. The intersection is functional — effective written/oral communication requires language knowledge. GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI lacks a foundational communication/language competency KSA.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of the structure and content of a foreign (non-English) language including the meaning and spelling of words, rules of composition and grammar, and pronunciation.
mappings.notes: Foreign language competency outside WIDAI scope. No gap — WIDAI is language-agnostic in its competency definitions.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of the theory and techniques required to compose, produce, and perform works of music, dance, visual arts, drama, and sculpture.
mappings.notes: Fine arts outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of historical events and their causes, indicators, and effects on civilizations and cultures.
mappings.notes: History/archeology outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of different philosophical systems and religions. This includes their basic principles, values, ethics, ways of thinking, customs, practices, and their impact on human culture.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
mappings.notes: Weak intersection: ethical philosophy informs AI ethics and responsible AI principles. O*NET covers broad philosophy/theology. WIDAI covers applied AI ethics. The ethical reasoning foundations intersect.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of relevant equipment, policies, procedures, and strategies to promote effective local, state, or national security operations for the protection of people, data, property, and institutions.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of information security principles, including confidentiality, integrity, and availability (CIA triad), defense in depth, least privilege, and how these principles apply to data architectures and AI systems.
mappings.notes: O*NET covers all security domains including physical. WIDAI SP domain covers data/information security specifically. Strong intersection in data protection principles and security strategies. Secondary: SP-K-002 (access control), SP-K-003 (threat landscape), SP-K-004 (encryption).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of laws, legal codes, court procedures, precedents, government regulations, executive orders, agency rules, and the democratic political process.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of major data protection regulations including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, PIPA, and PIPL, including their scope, legal bases for processing, data subject rights, cross-border transfer mechanisms, and enforcement frameworks.
mappings.notes: O*NET covers all law and government. WIDAI RC domain covers data protection and AI regulation specifically but in much greater depth. Multiple WIDAI KSAs fall within this: RC-K-001 through RC-K-014 cover specific regulatory frameworks. O*NET is a superset at the general level; WIDAI has deeper domain-specific coverage.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of transmission, broadcasting, switching, control, and operation of telecommunications systems.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, VPNs, firewalls, load balancers, and how network architecture affects data pipeline performance and security.
mappings.notes: Partial overlap: telecommunications infrastructure underlies network fundamentals. WIDAI TF-K-013 covers networking for data systems specifically. O*NET is broader (broadcasting, switching).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of media production, communication, and dissemination techniques and methods. This includes alternative ways to inform and entertain via written, oral, and visual media.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data visualization best practices, including chart type selection, cognitive load principles, color accessibility, and the difference between exploratory and explanatory visualization.
mappings.notes: Weak intersection: communication and visual media principles overlap with data visualization and stakeholder communication. O*NET covers broad media. WIDAI covers data-specific communication through visualization and reporting.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of principles and methods for moving people or goods by air, rail, sea, or road, including the relative costs and benefits.
mappings.notes: Transportation knowledge outside WIDAI scope. No gap.

mappings.fde_description: Understanding written sentences and paragraphs in work-related documents.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
mappings.notes: O*NET reading comprehension is a foundational skill underlying many WIDAI KSAs but not directly defined in WIDAI. All WIDAI roles require reading comprehension for documentation, regulations, technical specifications. GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI lacks foundational communication skills as standalone KSAs. Functional rationale: the outcome (understanding written content) is prerequisite to virtually all WIDAI tasks.

mappings.fde_description: Giving full attention to what other people are saying, taking time to understand the points being made, asking questions as appropriate, and not interrupting at inappropriate times.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in identifying, mapping, and engaging stakeholders across the organization for data and AI initiatives, including influence assessment, communication strategy design, and resistance management.
mappings.notes: Active listening underlies stakeholder engagement. WIDAI covers the applied outcome (stakeholder management) but not the foundational interpersonal skill. GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI lacks foundational interpersonal/communication skills.

mappings.fde_description: Communicating effectively in writing as appropriate for the needs of the audience.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in producing analysis documentation that is reproducible, clearly reasoned, and accessible to a non-technical business audience.
mappings.notes: O*NET writing is general; WIDAI has multiple writing-adjacent skills: AB-S-002 (analysis documentation), AG-S-008 (AI documentation), AB-T-003 (methodology documentation). The functional outcome overlaps but WIDAI focuses on domain-specific writing contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Talking to others to convey information effectively.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
mappings.notes: O*NET speaking is general. WIDAI LS-S-006 is board presentations specifically. Multiple WIDAI KSAs involve speaking (stakeholder communication, team leadership, mentoring). WIDAI covers domain-specific speaking contexts rather than the foundational skill.

mappings.fde_description: Using mathematics to solve problems.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing analytical studies that produce defensible causal or correlational conclusions, with appropriate documentation of assumptions and limitations.
mappings.notes: O*NET math skill is general. WIDAI has extensive applied math skills: AB-S-007 (study design), AB-K-008 (statistical modeling), AI-S-001 (ML implementation). The applied use of mathematics is well-covered across WIDAI; the general mathematical skill is a superset.

mappings.fde_description: Using scientific rules and methods to solve problems.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of A/B testing and experimentation design, including sample size calculation, randomization methods, guardrail metric selection, and statistical significance interpretation.
mappings.notes: Scientific method principles underlie WIDAI experimental design and analytical methodology. WIDAI covers specific applied scientific methods (A/B testing, causal inference, ML experimentation) but not the general scientific reasoning skill.

mappings.fde_description: Using logic and reasoning to identify the strengths and weaknesses of alternative solutions, conclusions, or approaches to problems.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to apply risk assessment methodologies proportionally, matching the depth and rigor of risk analysis to the actual materiality and likelihood of the risk rather than applying uniform assessment intensity.
mappings.notes: Critical thinking is a foundational cognitive skill underlying virtually every WIDAI Ability. WIDAI Abilities (RM-A-001, AG-A-001, DQ-A-001, AB-A-001, etc.) are all domain-specific expressions of critical thinking applied to data/AI contexts. O*NET is the general capability; WIDAI defines the applied expressions.

mappings.fde_description: Understanding the implications of new information for both current and future problem-solving and decision-making.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in evaluating emerging technologies and methodologies for data and AI, including readiness assessment, proof-of-concept design, and build-vs-buy analysis.
mappings.notes: Active learning — applying new knowledge to decisions — is expressed in WIDAI through emerging technology evaluation, continuous learning expectations, and knowledge currency. WIDAI LS-S-010 specifically covers evaluating new information/technology. GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI lacks a standalone 'continuous learning' or 'knowledge currency' KSA.

mappings.fde_description: Selecting and using training/instructional methods and procedures appropriate for the situation when learning or teaching new things.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data literacy program design, including competency assessment, curriculum development, delivery modalities, and measurement of literacy improvement across organizational roles.
mappings.notes: Strong overlap: WIDAI LS-K-012 directly covers training/instructional design for data literacy. O*NET is broader (all learning/teaching contexts). WIDAI also covers mentoring (LS-S-007, AB-S-004) which involves instructional skills.

mappings.fde_description: Monitoring/Assessing performance of yourself, other individuals, or organizations to make improvements or take corrective action.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in monitoring data pipeline execution, identifying failure patterns, and implementing corrective actions that address root causes rather than symptoms.
mappings.notes: O*NET monitoring covers all performance monitoring. WIDAI covers system/pipeline monitoring (OP-S-006), data quality monitoring (DQ-T-001), model performance monitoring (AI-T-005), and organizational performance. Multiple intersections across domains.

mappings.fde_description: Being aware of others' reactions and understanding why they react as they do.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in identifying, mapping, and engaging stakeholders across the organization for data and AI initiatives, including influence assessment, communication strategy design, and resistance management.
mappings.notes: Social perceptiveness underlies stakeholder management and change management. WIDAI LS-S-005 requires reading stakeholder reactions; LS-K-009 covers organizational behavior. The functional outcome of reading people's reactions is embedded in WIDAI leadership KSAs.

mappings.fde_description: Adjusting actions in relation to others' actions.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in building cross-functional coalitions for data and AI initiatives, including identifying shared objectives, negotiating resource commitments, and maintaining collaborative relationships across organizational boundaries.
mappings.notes: Coordination underlies all WIDAI cross-functional work: LS-S-003 (coalition building), AB-T-004 (cross-functional analytical projects), OP-T-003 (cross-team coordination). WIDAI covers the applied contexts; O*NET defines the general skill.

mappings.fde_description: Persuading others to change their minds or behavior.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
mappings.notes: Persuasion is embedded in WIDAI leadership KSAs: board presentations (LS-S-006), stakeholder engagement (LS-S-005), building data culture (LS-K-012). WIDAI doesn't define persuasion as standalone but operationalizes it across contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Bringing others together and trying to reconcile differences.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in building cross-functional coalitions for data and AI initiatives, including identifying shared objectives, negotiating resource commitments, and maintaining collaborative relationships across organizational boundaries.
mappings.notes: Negotiation explicitly mentioned in LS-S-003 (negotiating resource commitments). Also relevant to vendor management (OP-S-003), stakeholder alignment. WIDAI embeds negotiation within applied contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Teaching others how to do something.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in mentoring and developing data and AI professionals, including individualized development planning, technical coaching, career guidance, and creating growth opportunities within the organizational context.
mappings.notes: Strong overlap with WIDAI mentoring/teaching KSAs: LS-S-007 (mentoring), AB-S-004 (mentoring junior analysts), LS-K-012 (data literacy program design), OP-K-010 (community of practice facilitation).

mappings.fde_description: Actively looking for ways to help people.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of self-service data platform design principles, including data marketplace concepts, data catalog usability, access request workflows, and user experience considerations that drive adoption.
mappings.notes: Weak functional overlap. Service orientation as a disposition is not directly defined in WIDAI. WIDAI covers service delivery (self-service platforms, data product design, service catalogs) but not the underlying service mindset. GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI lacks a service orientation or customer-centricity ability.

mappings.fde_description: Identifying complex problems and reviewing related information to develop and evaluate options and implement solutions.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to synthesize risk information from multiple sources and assessment methodologies into coherent risk narratives that support executive decision-making.
mappings.notes: Complex problem solving is the core cognitive skill underlying all WIDAI analytical and decision-making KSAs. WIDAI expresses it through domain-specific problem-solving: risk synthesis (RM-A-003), analytical study design (AB-S-007), architecture trade-off analysis (DA-K-001), governance calibration (AG-A-001). O*NET is the general capability.

mappings.fde_description: Analyzing needs and product requirements to create a design.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in translating business requirements into data architecture specifications, including dimensional models, integration patterns, and API designs that serve both current and anticipated analytical needs.
mappings.notes: Strong overlap. WIDAI DA-S-001 is the data-specific expression of operations analysis — translating requirements into designs. Also: OP-S-001 (DataOps design), DQ-A-004 (data product design). The analytical-to-design pipeline is well-covered in WIDAI.

mappings.fde_description: Generating or adapting equipment and technology to serve user needs.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in translating business requirements into data architecture specifications, including dimensional models, integration patterns, and API designs that serve both current and anticipated analytical needs.
mappings.notes: O*NET technology design maps to WIDAI architecture and platform design skills. DA-S-001 (architecture specs), DA-K-004 (platform architecture), OP-K-005 (self-service platform design). WIDAI covers data technology design specifically.

mappings.fde_description: Determining the kind of tools and equipment needed to do a job.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in evaluating data and AI technology vendors, including capability assessment, total cost of ownership analysis, integration complexity evaluation, and vendor risk assessment.
mappings.notes: Functional equivalent: selecting data/AI tools and platforms. WIDAI OP-S-003 (vendor evaluation), LS-S-010 (emerging tech evaluation), LS-K-006 (vendor ecosystem knowledge) cover the data/AI technology selection process.

mappings.fde_description: Installing equipment, machines, wiring, or programs to meet specifications.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in containerization and orchestration using Docker and Kubernetes, including building container images, writing Kubernetes manifests, managing deployments, and troubleshooting container runtime issues.
mappings.notes: Functional overlap: installing/deploying software systems. WIDAI covers software deployment (TF-S-009 containerization, TF-S-008 CI/CD) rather than physical installation. The functional outcome — setting up systems to specification — intersects.

mappings.fde_description: Writing computer programs for various purposes.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in writing Python code for data processing, analysis, and machine learning workflows, including data manipulation with pandas, numerical computation with NumPy, and implementing ML pipelines.
mappings.notes: Strong overlap. WIDAI TF-S-001 (Python programming), TF-S-002 (SQL), TF-S-010 (API development) directly cover programming. O*NET is broader (all programming); WIDAI covers data/AI-specific programming languages and contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Watching gauges, dials, or other indicators to make sure a machine is working properly.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in monitoring data pipeline execution, identifying failure patterns, and implementing corrective actions that address root causes rather than symptoms.
mappings.notes: Functional equivalent: monitoring system health indicators. WIDAI OP-S-006 (pipeline monitoring), AB-T-011 (pipeline health dashboards), AI-T-005 (model monitoring). O*NET focuses on physical indicators; WIDAI on data system observability.

mappings.fde_description: Controlling operations of equipment or systems.
mappings.ref_statement: Operate and maintain data pipeline orchestration systems, including scheduling configuration, dependency management, failure handling, retry logic, and alerting.
mappings.notes: Functional equivalent: operating data/AI systems. WIDAI OP domain covers data operations extensively. O*NET focuses on equipment/systems broadly; WIDAI on data infrastructure operations.

mappings.fde_description: Performing routine maintenance on equipment and determining when and what kind of maintenance is needed.
mappings.ref_statement: Perform routine operational maintenance on data infrastructure, including storage optimization, index maintenance, backup verification, and access review.
mappings.notes: Strong functional match. WIDAI OP-T-007 directly covers routine maintenance of data infrastructure — the digital equivalent of equipment maintenance. Also: AB-T-007 (BI asset maintenance).

mappings.fde_description: Determining causes of operating errors and deciding what to do about it.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in monitoring data pipeline execution, identifying failure patterns, and implementing corrective actions that address root causes rather than symptoms.
mappings.notes: Strong functional match. WIDAI OP-S-006 explicitly covers root cause analysis and corrective action — the data systems expression of troubleshooting. Also: TF-S-009 (container troubleshooting), DQ-S-001 (data quality issue root cause).

mappings.fde_description: Repairing machines or systems using the needed tools.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in monitoring data pipeline execution, identifying failure patterns, and implementing corrective actions that address root causes rather than symptoms.
mappings.notes: Functional overlap: repairing data systems after failures. WIDAI covers corrective action and remediation but 'repairing' as a physical skill doesn't directly translate. The functional outcome — restoring systems to working state — intersects.

mappings.fde_description: Conducting tests and inspections of products, services, or processes to evaluate quality or performance.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in profiling datasets to assess completeness, uniqueness, distribution patterns, and referential integrity, and in using profiling results to prioritize data quality improvement efforts.
mappings.notes: Strong overlap. WIDAI DQ domain is essentially quality control applied to data. DQ-S-001 (data profiling), DQ-T-001 (monitoring), DQ-K-001 (quality dimensions), AB-S-009 (pipeline testing). WIDAI has deep coverage of data quality control.

mappings.fde_description: Considering the relative costs and benefits of potential actions to choose the most appropriate one.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to apply risk assessment methodologies proportionally, matching the depth and rigor of risk analysis to the actual materiality and likelihood of the risk rather than applying uniform assessment intensity.
mappings.notes: Judgment and decision-making is foundational across WIDAI. Multiple Abilities express domain-specific judgment: RM-A-001 (proportional risk assessment), AG-A-001 (governance calibration), DQ-A-001 (impact-based prioritization), AB-A-002 (method selection). O*NET is the general capability; WIDAI operationalizes it extensively.

mappings.fde_description: Determining how a system should work and how changes in conditions, operations, and the environment will affect outcomes.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data architecture design principles, including data modeling techniques (conceptual, logical, physical), schema design patterns (star, snowflake, Data Vault), and the trade-offs between different modeling approaches for analytical versus operational workloads.
mappings.notes: Systems analysis maps to WIDAI architecture and design KSAs. DA-K-001 (architecture design), DA-K-004 (platform architecture), DA-S-001 (translating requirements). WIDAI covers systems analysis applied to data/AI systems specifically.

mappings.fde_description: Identifying measures or indicators of system performance and the actions needed to improve or correct performance, relative to the goals of the system.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in monitoring data pipeline execution, identifying failure patterns, and implementing corrective actions that address root causes rather than symptoms.
mappings.notes: Systems evaluation maps to WIDAI operational monitoring and performance assessment. OP-S-006 (pipeline monitoring/correction), AI-T-005 (model performance monitoring), DQ-T-001 (quality monitoring). WIDAI has specific evaluation skills for data/AI systems.

mappings.fde_description: Managing one's own time and the time of others.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in developing and executing multi-year data and AI strategies, including vision articulation, milestone definition, resource planning, and adaptive replanning in response to organizational changes.
mappings.notes: Time management is embedded in WIDAI project/strategy execution KSAs but not defined as a standalone skill. LS-S-001 involves timeline management. GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI lacks explicit project/time management competencies.

mappings.fde_description: Determining how money will be spent to get the work done, and accounting for these expenditures.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of operational budgeting for data and AI functions, including cost modeling for cloud infrastructure, vendor licensing, talent acquisition, and the financial frameworks used to justify data and AI investments to finance leadership.
mappings.notes: Strong overlap. WIDAI LS-K-008 directly covers financial resource management for data/AI. Also: OP-K-004 (operational budgeting), LS-K-010 (data monetization). WIDAI covers financial management specifically for data/AI functions.

mappings.fde_description: Obtaining and seeing to the appropriate use of equipment, facilities, and materials needed to do certain work.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of operational budgeting and resource allocation for data operations, including infrastructure cost modeling, vendor contract management, capacity planning, and the financial metrics used to demonstrate operational efficiency.
mappings.notes: Functional overlap: managing data infrastructure resources (cloud compute, storage, licensing) is the digital equivalent of material resource management. WIDAI OP-K-004 and OP-T-009 (capacity planning) cover this.

mappings.fde_description: Motivating, developing, and directing people as they work, identifying the best people for the job.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in mentoring and developing data and AI professionals, including individualized development planning, technical coaching, career guidance, and creating growth opportunities within the organizational context.
mappings.notes: Strong overlap. WIDAI LS domain covers personnel management for data/AI teams extensively: LS-S-007 (mentoring/development), LS-K-002 (talent management), LS-S-002 (talent pipeline), LS-K-013 (workforce planning), LS-S-004 (team building).

mappings.fde_description: The ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in identifying, mapping, and engaging stakeholders across the organization for data and AI initiatives, including influence assessment, communication strategy design, and resistance management.
mappings.notes: Foundational cognitive ability underlying all WIDAI interpersonal KSAs. Not directly defined in WIDAI — assumed as prerequisite. GAP consideration: not a gap because WIDAI defines professional competencies, not foundational cognitive abilities.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writing.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of major data protection regulations including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, PIPA, and PIPL, including their scope, legal bases for processing, data subject rights, cross-border transfer mechanisms, and enforcement frameworks.
mappings.notes: Foundational ability required to comprehend regulatory text, technical documentation, etc. Prerequisite across all WIDAI domains. Not an WIDAI gap — cognitive prerequisite, not professional competency.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
mappings.notes: WIDAI LS-S-006 is a domain-specific expression of oral expression ability. Multiple WIDAI KSAs involve speaking (stakeholder engagement, team leadership, consultation). O*NET defines the general cognitive ability.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in producing analysis documentation that is reproducible, clearly reasoned, and accessible to a non-technical business audience.
mappings.notes: WIDAI has multiple written expression KSAs: AB-S-002 (analysis docs), AG-S-008 (AI documentation), AB-T-003 (methodology docs), AB-T-010 (regulatory-standard docs). All are domain-specific expressions of written expression.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to come up with a number of ideas about a topic (the number of ideas is important, not their quality, correctness, or creativity).
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in evaluating emerging technologies and methodologies for data and AI, including readiness assessment, proof-of-concept design, and build-vs-buy analysis.
mappings.notes: Fluency of ideas is a cognitive ability that supports innovation and strategy development. WIDAI doesn't directly measure idea generation as a standalone competency. Weakly reflected in technology evaluation and strategy development KSAs.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to come up with unusual or clever ideas about a given topic or situation, or to develop creative ways to solve a problem.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in translating business requirements into data architecture specifications, including dimensional models, integration patterns, and API designs that serve both current and anticipated analytical needs.
mappings.notes: Originality/creativity is an underlying cognitive ability. WIDAI architecture and design KSAs require creative problem-solving but don't define it as a standalone ability. Not an WIDAI gap — cognitive trait vs. professional competency.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to tell when something is wrong or is likely to go wrong. It does not involve solving the problem, only recognizing that there is a problem.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to apply risk assessment methodologies proportionally, matching the depth and rigor of risk analysis to the actual materiality and likelihood of the risk rather than applying uniform assessment intensity.
mappings.notes: Problem sensitivity maps to WIDAI risk identification and monitoring abilities. RM-A-001 (proportional assessment), OP-S-006 (failure pattern identification), DQ-S-001 (data quality issue identification). WIDAI operationalizes problem detection in domain-specific contexts.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to apply general rules to specific problems to produce answers that make sense.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in conducting Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) for AI systems, including risk identification, necessity and proportionality analysis, stakeholder consultation, and mitigation measure design.
mappings.notes: Deductive reasoning underlies all WIDAI analytical and compliance work — applying regulatory rules to specific cases (RC), applying governance frameworks to specific AI systems (AG), applying data quality rules to specific datasets (DQ). Foundational cognitive ability expressed across WIDAI domains.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to combine pieces of information to form general rules or conclusions (includes finding a relationship among seemingly unrelated events).
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to identify and communicate when analytical findings challenge prevailing business narratives, presenting disconfirming evidence constructively and with appropriate context.
mappings.notes: Inductive reasoning is the cognitive foundation of data analysis — drawing conclusions from data. WIDAI analytical abilities (AB-A-001 through AB-A-003) and AI/ML KSAs (pattern recognition, model development) are domain-specific expressions of inductive reasoning.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to arrange things or actions in a certain order or pattern according to a specific rule or set of rules.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing data classification schemes that balance organizational needs for data access with protection requirements, and that can be practically implemented and maintained across diverse data environments.
mappings.notes: Information ordering maps to WIDAI classification, taxonomy design, and data organization skills. DG-S-001 (classification schemes), DA-K-001 (data modeling), DQ-K-001 (quality dimensions). Organizing information according to rules is core to data governance.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to generate or use different sets of rules for combining or grouping things in different ways.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to adapt risk management frameworks developed for traditional data and analytics to emerging AI/ML contexts, recognizing where established approaches apply directly, where they need modification, and where entirely new frameworks are required.
mappings.notes: Category flexibility — applying different frameworks to the same problem — is expressed in WIDAI through framework adaptation (RM-A-004), governance calibration (AG-A-001), and analytical method selection (AB-A-002).

mappings.fde_description: The ability to choose the right mathematical methods or formulas to solve a problem.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
mappings.notes: Strong match. WIDAI AB-A-002 is mathematical reasoning applied to data analysis — selecting the right method for the problem. Also: AB-K-008 (statistical modeling methods), AI-K-003 (probability/statistics).

mappings.fde_description: The ability to add, subtract, multiply, or divide quickly and correctly.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of descriptive statistics, including measures of central tendency, dispersion, distribution shape, and correlation, and how to interpret each in a business context.
mappings.notes: Basic arithmetic facility is prerequisite but not a differentiating data/AI competency. WIDAI assumes numerical facility as a baseline. Not an WIDAI gap — cognitive prerequisite.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to remember information such as words, numbers, pictures, and procedures.
mappings.notes: Memorization is a basic cognitive ability not differentiating for data/AI professional competency. WIDAI appropriately does not include it. Not a gap.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to quickly make sense of, combine, and organize information into meaningful patterns.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to synthesize risk information from multiple sources and assessment methodologies into coherent risk narratives that support executive decision-making.
mappings.notes: Pattern recognition and information synthesis maps to WIDAI analytical and risk synthesis abilities. RM-A-003 (risk synthesis), AB-A-001 (challenging narratives from data patterns). The cognitive ability underlies domain-specific pattern recognition.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to identify or detect a known pattern that is hidden in other distracting material.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in profiling datasets to assess completeness, uniqueness, distribution patterns, and referential integrity, and in using profiling results to prioritize data quality improvement efforts.
mappings.notes: Detecting hidden patterns in noisy data is core to data quality and analytics. DQ-S-001 (data profiling), AI-K-003 (pattern recognition in ML). Cognitive ability expressed in data-specific contexts.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to quickly and accurately compare similarities and differences among sets of letters, numbers, objects, pictures, or patterns.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in profiling datasets to assess completeness, uniqueness, distribution patterns, and referential integrity, and in using profiling results to prioritize data quality improvement efforts.
mappings.notes: Comparing data elements for consistency and accuracy is part of data quality work. Weak intersection — modern data work relies more on automated comparison than manual perceptual speed.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to know your location in relation to the environment or to know where other objects are in relation to you.
mappings.notes: Physical spatial orientation not relevant to data/AI knowledge work. Not a gap.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to imagine how something will look after it is moved around or when its parts are moved or rearranged.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data architecture design principles, including data modeling techniques (conceptual, logical, physical), schema design patterns (star, snowflake, Data Vault), and the trade-offs between different modeling approaches for analytical versus operational workloads.
mappings.notes: Mental visualization of system architectures and data flows requires this cognitive ability. WIDAI architecture design KSAs benefit from visualization ability. Also relevant to AB-K-002 (data visualization design). Functional intersection rather than direct competency.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to concentrate on a task over a period of time without being distracted.
mappings.notes: Selective attention is a basic cognitive trait, not a differentiating professional competency. WIDAI appropriately does not define it. Not a gap.

mappings.fde_description: The ability to shift back and forth between two or more activities or sources of information.
mappings.notes: Cognitive multitasking is a basic ability, not a professional competency. Not a gap for WIDAI.

mappings.fde_description: Observing, receiving, and otherwise obtaining information from all relevant sources.
mappings.ref_statement: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
mappings.notes: WIDAI analytical tasks inherently involve getting information. AB-T-001 (data extraction), DQ-T-001 (data monitoring), OP-T-001 (requirements gathering). WIDAI covers information acquisition in domain-specific contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Monitoring and reviewing information from materials, events, or the environment, to detect or assess problems.
mappings.ref_statement: Operate and maintain data pipeline orchestration systems, including scheduling configuration, dependency management, failure handling, retry logic, and alerting.
mappings.notes: Process monitoring is core to WIDAI operations. OP-T-006 (pipeline orchestration), AI-T-005 (model monitoring), DQ-T-001 (data quality monitoring), AB-T-011 (pipeline health). Strong functional overlap.

mappings.fde_description: Identifying information by categorizing, estimating, recognizing differences or similarities, and detecting changes in circumstances or events.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing data classification schemes that balance organizational needs for data access with protection requirements, and that can be practically implemented and maintained across diverse data environments.
mappings.notes: Categorizing and classifying information maps to WIDAI data governance and quality: DG-S-001 (classification), DQ-S-001 (data profiling), SP-S-007 (data classification frameworks).

mappings.fde_description: Inspecting equipment, structures, or materials to identify the cause of errors or other problems or defects.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in profiling datasets to assess completeness, uniqueness, distribution patterns, and referential integrity, and in using profiling results to prioritize data quality improvement efforts.
mappings.notes: Functional equivalent: inspecting data assets for defects. DQ-S-001 (data profiling), SP-S-008 (security assessments). WIDAI covers 'inspection' of data systems rather than physical equipment.

mappings.fde_name: Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
mappings.fde_description: Estimating sizes, distances, and quantities; or determining time, costs, resources, or materials needed to perform a work activity.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of operational budgeting and resource allocation for data operations, including infrastructure cost modeling, vendor contract management, capacity planning, and the financial metrics used to demonstrate operational efficiency.
mappings.notes: Estimating costs, resources, and quantities maps to WIDAI capacity planning and budgeting. OP-K-004 (budgeting), OP-T-009 (capacity planning), LS-K-008 (investment budgeting).

mappings.fde_name: Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
mappings.fde_description: Assessing the value, importance, or quality of things or people.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to prioritize data quality improvement efforts based on downstream business impact rather than technical severity alone, connecting quality investment decisions to measurable business outcomes.
mappings.notes: Quality judgment maps to WIDAI quality assessment, vendor evaluation, and talent assessment. DQ-A-001 (quality prioritization), OP-S-003 (vendor evaluation), LS-K-002 (talent assessment).

mappings.fde_description: Compiling, coding, categorizing, calculating, tabulating, auditing, or verifying information or data.
mappings.ref_statement: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
mappings.notes: Core overlap. Processing information is the fundamental activity of data professionals. WIDAI covers this extensively: AB-T-001 (analysis execution), DQ tasks (data quality processing), TF-S-001 (data processing with Python), TF-S-002 (SQL queries).

mappings.fde_name: Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
mappings.fde_description: Using relevant information and individual judgment to determine whether events or processes comply with laws, regulations, or standards.
mappings.ref_statement: Conduct regulatory compliance assessments for data processing activities, including gap analysis against applicable regulations, documentation of findings, and development of remediation recommendations.
mappings.notes: Very strong match. WIDAI RC domain is built around compliance evaluation. RC-T-001 (compliance assessments), AG-T-001 (AI governance assessments), DQ-T-001 (quality standard compliance). WIDAI has deep compliance evaluation coverage.

mappings.fde_description: Identifying the underlying principles, reasons, or facts of information by breaking down information or data into separate parts.
mappings.ref_statement: Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
mappings.notes: Near-exact match. Data analysis is WIDAI's core domain. The entire AB domain, plus AI analytical tasks, DQ profiling, and RM risk analysis. O*NET describes the general activity; WIDAI defines it in extensive depth across multiple specializations.

mappings.fde_description: Analyzing information and evaluating results to choose the best solution and solve problems.
mappings.ref_statement: Ability to apply risk assessment methodologies proportionally, matching the depth and rigor of risk analysis to the actual materiality and likelihood of the risk rather than applying uniform assessment intensity.
mappings.notes: Decision-making and problem-solving pervade all WIDAI domains. Every WIDAI Ability is a domain-specific expression of this work activity. O*NET describes the general activity; WIDAI operationalizes it across data, AI, governance, risk, compliance contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Developing, designing, or creating new applications, ideas, relationships, systems, or products, including artistic contributions.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in translating business requirements into data architecture specifications, including dimensional models, integration patterns, and API designs that serve both current and anticipated analytical needs.
mappings.notes: Creative design is embedded in WIDAI architecture and product design KSAs. DA-S-001 (architecture design), DQ-A-004 (data product design), LS-S-010 (emerging tech evaluation). WIDAI covers creative thinking in applied technical contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Keeping up-to-date technically and applying new knowledge to your job.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in evaluating emerging technologies and methodologies for data and AI, including readiness assessment, proof-of-concept design, and build-vs-buy analysis.
mappings.notes: Knowledge currency maps to WIDAI emerging tech evaluation and continuous learning expectations. LS-S-010 (emerging tech), AG-A-004 (translating new research into policy). GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI could benefit from a standalone 'continuous professional development' or 'knowledge currency' KSA.

mappings.fde_description: Establishing long-range objectives and specifying the strategies and actions to achieve them.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in developing and executing multi-year data and AI strategies, including vision articulation, milestone definition, resource planning, and adaptive replanning in response to organizational changes.
mappings.notes: Very strong match. WIDAI LS-S-001 is a direct domain-specific expression of this activity. Also: LS-K-001 (strategic planning knowledge), LS-T-001 (strategy development task). WIDAI covers strategic planning extensively for data/AI.

mappings.fde_description: Scheduling events, programs, and activities, as well as the work of others.
mappings.ref_statement: Operate and maintain data pipeline orchestration systems, including scheduling configuration, dependency management, failure handling, retry logic, and alerting.
mappings.notes: Scheduling maps to both pipeline orchestration (OP-T-006) and project management. WIDAI covers technical scheduling (pipelines) and strategic planning (LS-S-001) but not general project scheduling as standalone. Weak gap signal for project management.

mappings.fde_description: Developing specific goals and plans to prioritize, organize, and accomplish your work.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in developing and executing multi-year data and AI strategies, including vision articulation, milestone definition, resource planning, and adaptive replanning in response to organizational changes.
mappings.notes: Planning and prioritization mapped across WIDAI leadership and operations. LS-S-001 (strategy), DQ-A-001 (quality prioritization), RM-A-001 (proportional assessment). WIDAI covers planning in domain contexts but lacks standalone project management KSA.

mappings.fde_description: Using computers and computer systems (including hardware and software) to program, write software, set up functions, enter data, or process information.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in writing Python code for data processing, analysis, and machine learning workflows, including data manipulation with pandas, numerical computation with NumPy, and implementing ML pipelines.
mappings.notes: WIDAI TF domain is entirely within this O*NET activity. TF-S-001 (Python), TF-S-002 (SQL), TF-S-009 (containers), plus all analytical and engineering tasks that use computers. WIDAI defines domain-specific computer work in extensive depth.

mappings.fde_name: Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
mappings.fde_description: Providing documentation, detailed instructions, drawings, or specifications to tell others about how devices, parts, equipment, or structures are to be fabricated, constructed, assembled, modified, maintained, or used.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in translating business requirements into data architecture specifications, including dimensional models, integration patterns, and API designs that serve both current and anticipated analytical needs.
mappings.notes: Functional equivalent: creating technical specifications for data systems. DA-S-001 (architecture specs), TF-S-010 (API design). WIDAI covers technical specification writing for data/AI systems.

mappings.fde_description: Servicing, repairing, adjusting, and testing machines, devices, moving parts, and equipment that operate primarily on the basis of mechanical principles.
mappings.notes: Physical mechanical maintenance outside WIDAI scope. Not a gap.

mappings.fde_description: Servicing, repairing, calibrating, regulating, fine-tuning, or testing machines, devices, and equipment that operate primarily on the basis of electrical or electronic principles.
mappings.ref_statement: Perform routine operational maintenance on data infrastructure, including storage optimization, index maintenance, backup verification, and access review.
mappings.notes: Weak functional overlap: maintaining electronic data infrastructure. WIDAI covers software/system maintenance rather than hardware electronics. Tangential intersection.

mappings.fde_description: Entering, transcribing, recording, storing, or maintaining information in written or electronic/magnetic form.
mappings.ref_statement: Document analytical methodologies, data sources, business rules, and known data limitations for all recurring analyses and reports.
mappings.notes: Documentation is pervasive in WIDAI. AB-T-003 (methodology documentation), AG-S-008 (AI documentation), AB-T-010 (regulatory documentation), RC-T-002 (compliance documentation). WIDAI covers domain-specific documentation extensively.

mappings.fde_name: Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
mappings.fde_description: Translating or explaining what information means and how it can be used.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
mappings.notes: Strong match. Translation of complex information is a core WIDAI leadership competency. LS-S-006 (board presentations), AB-S-002 (accessible documentation), AB-A-001 (communicating challenging findings). WIDAI covers interpretation and translation extensively.

mappings.fde_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
mappings.fde_description: Providing information to supervisors, co-workers, and subordinates by telephone, in written form, e-mail, or in person.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in identifying, mapping, and engaging stakeholders across the organization for data and AI initiatives, including influence assessment, communication strategy design, and resistance management.
mappings.notes: Internal communication underlies all WIDAI collaborative work. LS-S-005 (stakeholder engagement), LS-S-003 (cross-functional coalitions), LS-S-007 (mentoring). WIDAI covers communication in applied leadership contexts.

mappings.fde_name: Communicating with People Outside the Organization
mappings.fde_description: Communicating with people outside the organization, representing the organization to customers, the public, government, and other external sources.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in engaging with regulatory bodies during examinations, audits, and inquiries, including preparation of required documentation, coordination of response teams, and management of information requests.
mappings.notes: External communication maps to WIDAI regulatory engagement (RC-S-010), vendor management (OP-S-003), and board presentations (LS-S-006). WIDAI covers external communication in specific professional contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Developing constructive and cooperative working relationships with others, and maintaining them over time.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in building cross-functional coalitions for data and AI initiatives, including identifying shared objectives, negotiating resource commitments, and maintaining collaborative relationships across organizational boundaries.
mappings.notes: Relationship building is core to WIDAI leadership. LS-S-003 (coalitions), LS-S-004 (team building), LS-S-005 (stakeholder engagement). WIDAI covers professional relationship building in data/AI organizational contexts.

mappings.fde_description: Providing personal assistance, medical attention, emotional support, or other personal care to others such as coworkers, customers, or patients.
mappings.notes: Personal care/medical assistance outside WIDAI scope. Not a gap.

mappings.fde_description: Convincing others to buy merchandise/goods or to otherwise change their minds or actions.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
mappings.notes: Influencing (not selling) maps to WIDAI leadership. LS-S-006 (board persuasion), LS-S-005 (stakeholder influence), AG-A-003 (building RAI commitment). WIDAI covers organizational influence but not commercial selling.

mappings.fde_name: Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
mappings.fde_description: Handling complaints, settling disputes, and resolving grievances and conflicts, or otherwise negotiating with others.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in building cross-functional coalitions for data and AI initiatives, including identifying shared objectives, negotiating resource commitments, and maintaining collaborative relationships across organizational boundaries.
mappings.notes: Conflict resolution and negotiation embedded in WIDAI coalition building (LS-S-003) and stakeholder management (LS-S-005). WIDAI covers negotiation in organizational data/AI contexts. GAP SIGNAL: WIDAI lacks explicit conflict resolution skills.

mappings.fde_name: Performing for or Working Directly with the Public
mappings.fde_description: Performing for people or dealing directly with the public.
mappings.notes: Public-facing performance outside WIDAI scope. Not a gap for data/AI professional roles.

mappings.fde_name: Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
mappings.fde_description: Getting members of a group to work together to accomplish tasks.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in building and leading high-performing data and AI teams, including role definition, hiring strategy, onboarding design, performance management, and creating team cultures that balance technical excellence with business orientation.
mappings.notes: Strong match. WIDAI leadership domain covers team coordination extensively. LS-S-004 (team building), LS-S-003 (cross-functional coordination), AB-T-004 (cross-functional project leadership).

mappings.fde_description: Encouraging and building mutual trust, respect, and cooperation among team members.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in building and leading high-performing data and AI teams, including role definition, hiring strategy, onboarding design, performance management, and creating team cultures that balance technical excellence with business orientation.
mappings.notes: Near-exact match. WIDAI LS-S-004 is the domain-specific expression of team development. Strong overlap in trust-building, culture creation, and team performance.

mappings.fde_description: Identifying the educational needs of others, developing formal educational or training programs or classes, and teaching or instructing others.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of data literacy program design, including competency assessment, curriculum development, delivery modalities, and measurement of literacy improvement across organizational roles.
mappings.notes: Strong match. WIDAI covers training through data literacy (LS-K-012), mentoring (LS-S-007, AB-S-004), and community facilitation (OP-K-010). All three O*NET sub-activities (needs identification, program development, teaching) are covered.

mappings.fde_description: Providing guidance and direction to subordinates, including setting performance standards and monitoring performance.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in building and leading high-performing data and AI teams, including role definition, hiring strategy, onboarding design, performance management, and creating team cultures that balance technical excellence with business orientation.
mappings.notes: WIDAI LS-S-004 explicitly covers performance management. Also: LS-S-007 (mentoring), LS-K-013 (workforce planning). WIDAI covers people leadership for data/AI teams comprehensively.

mappings.fde_description: Identifying the developmental needs of others and coaching, mentoring, or otherwise helping others to improve their knowledge or skills.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in mentoring and developing data and AI professionals, including individualized development planning, technical coaching, career guidance, and creating growth opportunities within the organizational context.
mappings.notes: Excellent match. WIDAI LS-S-007 is a direct, specific expression of coaching and development for data/AI professionals. Also: AB-S-004 (mentoring junior analysts). Very strong coverage.

mappings.fde_name: Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
mappings.fde_description: Providing guidance and expert advice to management or other groups on technical, systems-, or process-related topics.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in preparing and delivering board-level presentations on data and AI strategy, translating technical complexity into business language appropriate for governance audiences.
mappings.notes: Strong match. Consultation and advisory is a core WIDAI leadership function. LS-S-006 (board advisory), LS-S-005 (stakeholder guidance), RM-A-003 (risk advisory to executives). WIDAI covers expert consultation across data/AI domains.

mappings.fde_description: Performing day-to-day administrative tasks such as maintaining information files and processing paperwork.
mappings.notes: General administrative tasks outside WIDAI scope. Not a gap — WIDAI defines professional/technical competencies.

mappings.fde_description: Recruiting, interviewing, selecting, hiring, and promoting employees in an organization.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in building and sustaining talent pipelines for data and AI roles, including sourcing strategies, employer brand development, assessment design, and competitive positioning in tight labor markets.
mappings.notes: Strong match. WIDAI LS-S-002 (talent pipelines), LS-K-002 (talent management), LS-S-004 (hiring strategy). WIDAI covers staffing specifically for data/AI teams.

mappings.fde_description: Monitoring and controlling resources and overseeing the spending of money.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of operational budgeting for data and AI functions, including cost modeling for cloud infrastructure, vendor licensing, talent acquisition, and the financial frameworks used to justify data and AI investments to finance leadership.
mappings.notes: WIDAI covers resource management for data/AI functions. LS-K-008 (budgeting), OP-K-004 (operational budgeting), OP-T-009 (capacity planning). WIDAI has good coverage of financial and resource control.
